<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mano Kofun Cluster]][[ja:真野古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11583561]] Wikidata name: Mano Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mano Kofun Cluster | native_name =真野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mano Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|41|49.7|N|140|57|7.2|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|37.69713888888889|N|140.952|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Minamisoma|qid=Q642094}}| region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1947 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mano Kofun Cluster'''|真野古墳群| Mano Kofun-gun}} is a group of a approximately one hundred ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns located on [[:en:river terrace|river terrace]]s on the south coast of the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Some 27 of the tombs have been excavated. The site has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since October 24, 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137814|title=真野古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mano Kofun cluster extends over an area measuring three kilometers east-west by one kilometer north-south on the south shore of the Manogawa River, and were formerly grouped into the Hachimori Kofun cluster, Ōtani Kofun cluster and Koikebara Kofun cluster, but were made into a single grouping for the purposes of securing National Historic Site status. Full-scale excavations have been conducted since 1947, and a total of 27 units have been surveyed. Two of the larger tumuli have keyhole-shaped extensions to the top and bottom, and the largest has a moat and a length of 28.5 meters. Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/manokofunn-mappu.pdf Minamisōma town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Mano Kofun cluster extends over an area measuring three kilometers east-west by one kilometer north-south on the south shore of the Manogawa River, and were formerly grouped into the Hachimori Kofun cluster, Ōtani Kofun cluster and Koikebara Kofun cluster, but were made into a single grouping for the purposes of securing National Historic Site status. Full-scale excavations have been conducted since 1947, and a total of 27 units have been surveyed. Two of the larger tumuli have keyhole-shaped extensions to the top and bottom, and the largest has a moat and a length of 28.5 meters. Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

Some 27 of the tombs have been excavated. The site has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since October 24, 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137814|title=真野古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

Two of the larger tumuli have keyhole-shaped extensions to the top and bottom, and the largest has a moat and a length of 28.5 meters. Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Mano Kofun Cluster'''|真野古墳群| Mano Kofun-gun}} is a group of a approximately one hundred ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns located on [[:en:river terrace|river terrace]]s on the south coast of the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Some 27 of the tombs have been excavated. The site has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since October 24, 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137814|title=真野古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mano Kofun cluster extends over an area measuring three kilometers east-west by one kilometer north-south on the south shore of the Manogawa River, and were formerly grouped into the Hachimori Kofun cluster, Ōtani Kofun cluster and Koikebara Kofun cluster, but were made into a single grouping for the purposes of securing National Historic Site status. Full-scale excavations have been conducted since 1947, and a total of 27 units have been surveyed. Two of the larger tumuli have keyhole-shaped extensions to the top and bottom, and the largest has a moat and a length of 28.5 meters. Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial court]] during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kashima Station (Fukushima)|Kashima Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mano Kofun Cluster]][[ja:真野古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mano Kofun Cluster]][[ja:真野古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mano Kofun Cluster]][[ja:真野古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583561]] Wikidata name: Mano Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mano Kofun Cluster | native_name =真野古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mano Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 30 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|41|49.7|N|140|57|7.2|E||display=inline}}; {{Coord|37.69713888888889|N|140.952|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma, Fukushima]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Minamisoma|WD=Q642094}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1947 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Mano Kofun Cluster'''|真野古墳群| Mano Kofun-gun}} is a group of a approximately one hundred ''[[kofun]]'' Kofuns located on [[:en:river terrace|river terrace]]s on the south coast of the city of [[:en:Minamisōma, Fukushima|Minamisōma]], in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Some 27 of the tombs have been excavated. The site has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since October 24, 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137814|title=真野古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mano Kofun cluster extends over an area measuring three kilometers east-west by one kilometer north-south on the south shore of the Manogawa River, and were formerly grouped into the Hachimori Kofun cluster, Ōtani Kofun cluster and Koikebara Kofun cluster, but were made into a single grouping for the purposes of securing National Historic Site status. Full-scale excavations have been conducted since 1947, and a total of 27 units have been surveyed. Two of the larger tumuli have keyhole-shaped extensions to the top and bottom, and the largest has a moat and a length of 28.5 meters. Most of the others are small {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumuli with diameter of about 10 meters. The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial court}} during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
The site is approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kashima Station (Fukushima)|en|Kashima Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q6374317|lt=Kashima Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.minamisoma.lg.jp/material/files/group/43/manokofunn-mappu.pdf Minamisōma town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The internal structures of the excavated tombs exhibited a great variety, including flagstones, a box-type sarcophagus, and a pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included many straight swords, horse harnesses, and jade jewelry, and stone facsimiles of everyday objects such as axes, and sickles, belonging to the late Kofun period, or the 5th to 6th centuries AD. A number of the horse fittings were of bronze and gold imported from the Asian continent. As these objects were a monopoly of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial court|1=Imperial House of Japan}} during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].

The site has been protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since October 24, 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/137814|title=真野古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

As these objects were a monopoly of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial court|1=Imperial House of Japan}} during this period, these items indicate the burial of a powerful persons with a strong connection to the [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
